title: pedicularis rosea subsp. allionii
intro: Pedicularis rosea subsp. allionii, also known as Lousewort, is a plant species in the balsam family (Orobanchaceae) that obtains its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The health benefits of Pedicularis rosea subsp. allionii include reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and enhanced immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
